title: H001: Implement Keystone Operator packaging
summary: Review of fixes addressing external review #1 by berendt. Four findings were addressed: W-001 (conditional webhook Service port), W-002 (external-secrets dependency in HelmRelease), W-003 (Helm template syntax in docs), I-001 (remove -a flag from go build). Code changes are correct. However, the documentation was only partially updated — three sections in keystone-operator-packaging.md now diverge from the implementation.

tests_checklist.item: Helm lint passes (no Go/Python tests in scope — infrastructure packaging only)
helm template renders all resource types correctly

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing FluxCD HelmRelease pattern (interval, crds, remediation, dependsOn)
Follows SPDX header pattern on infrastructure files
Conditional webhook port consistent across service.yaml and deployment.yaml

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in Helm values or templates
ClusterRole follows least-privilege (matches kubebuilder markers exactly)
Pod security context: runAsNonRoot, drop ALL, readOnlyRootFilesystem, seccomp RuntimeDefault

architecture_checklist.item: HelmRelease dependsOn includes all required operators (cert-manager, mariadb, memcached, external-secrets)
CRD in crds/ directory identical to config/crd/bases/ source
All values.yaml parameters referenced in templates are defined

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unused values or template helpers
No over-engineered conditionals beyond what is needed

fail_fast_checklist.item: Webhook failurePolicy set to Fail (not Ignore)

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs handled (Helm values with sensible defaults)

issues_found.description: Dependency chain section not updated for external-secrets. Line 427 says 'three infrastructure operators' (should be four). The ASCII diagram (lines 431-437) omits external-secrets. The rationale table (lines 442-446) has no external-secrets row. The deployment sequence (lines 452-455) says 'all three' instead of 'all four'. The HelmRelease was updated (line 19-20) but the docs were not. This was explicitly requested in W-002: 'Also update the documentation dependency chain in docs/reference/backend/keystone-operator-packaging.md to include this dependency with its rationale.'
issues_found.fix: Add external-secrets to: (1) the introductory sentence (line 427: 'four infrastructure operators'), (2) the ASCII diagram (add external-secrets line under cert-manager and as a dependsOn under keystone-operator), (3) the rationale table (add row: external-secrets | external-secrets | Installs the external-secrets.io CRDs (ExternalSecret, PushSecret) that the Keystone operator creates to manage secrets lifecycle), (4) the deployment sequence (step 2: add external-secrets alongside mariadb/memcached; step 3: 'all four').

issues_found.description: Code snippet still shows 'go build -a -o manager main.go' but the Dockerfile (operators/keystone/Dockerfile:25) was changed to 'go build -o manager main.go' to address I-001. The documentation example now diverges from the implementation. Matches review pattern: cross-reference-documentation-examples-against-implementation.
issues_found.fix: Change line 79 from 'CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -o manager main.go' to 'CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o manager main.go'.

issues_found.description: Service Configuration section (lines 300-305) unconditionally lists the webhook port (443 to 9443) as always present. However, service.yaml (line 13) now wraps the webhook port in '{{- if .Values.webhook.enabled }}', making it conditional. The Deployment section (line 277) correctly documents the webhook port as conditional — the Service section should match. Matches review pattern: update-documentation-when-referenced-code-behavior-changes.
issues_found.fix: Update the Service Configuration table to add a 'Conditional' column (matching the Container Ports table format at line 273) and mark the webhook port as conditional on 'webhook.enabled'. Example: add '(conditional on webhook.enabled)' to the webhook row, or restructure the table to include a Conditional column.

next_steps: Fix DA3-001: Update the Dependency Chain section (lines 425-459) to include external-secrets in all four locations (intro, diagram, table, sequence)
Fix DA3-002: Remove '-a' flag from the code snippet at line 79
Fix DA3-003: Mark the webhook Service port as conditional in the Service Configuration section (lines 300-305)
